Carol Danvers — former US Air Force pilot with cosmic energy powers
He can shrink to microscopic size or grow to enormous size using a special suit

A subatomic dimension — key to time travel in Endgame

What is the Soul Stone's requirement?
400.a: You must sacrifice someone you love to obtain it

400.q: Who kills Thanos first in Endgame?
400.a: Thor — he decapitates the weakened Thanos in 2023

400.q: Significance of Tony's I am Iron Man in Endgame?
400.a: Echoes the first Iron Man film — here he uses all six Stones, sacrificing himself

400.q: Who is Kang the Conqueror?
400.a: A time-travelling conqueror — the primary villain of the Multiverse Saga

400.q: What are the Ten Rings?
400.a: An ancient organization led by the Mandarin — appears in Iron Man 3 and Shang-Chi

400.q: What is the Sokovia Accords?
400.a: A UN agreement requiring enhanced individuals to register under government oversight

400.q: What is the Raft?
400.a: A maximum-security underwater prison for superhumans

400.q: Who is Shuri?
400.a: T'Challa's sister and Wakanda's genius chief scientist

400.q: What is the Time Stone also called?
Who plays Nebula?
Name of the Guardians' ship?
Who is Moon Knight?
400.a: Marc Spector — a mercenary who becomes the avatar of Egyptian moon god Khonshu, with DID

400.q: What is the Multiverse Saga?
400.a: MCU Phases 4, 5, 6 — exploring alternate realities with Kang as the primary threat

400.q: Who is Miles Morales?
400.a: The second Spider-Man — a Black and Puerto Rican teenager from Brooklyn

400.q: What is the House of M storyline?
400.a: Wanda reshapes reality so mutants rule — she ends it saying No more mutants, depowering most X-Men

400.q: What is the Illuminati in Marvel comics?
400.a: A secret group including Iron Man, Mr. Fantastic, Professor X, Doctor Strange, Namor, Black Bolt

400.q: What is the Black Order?
400.a: Thanos's elite generals: Proxima Midnight, Corvus Glaive, Cull Obsidian, and Ebony Maw

400.q: What is the Kree-Skrull War?
400.a: An ancient interstellar war between two alien races — backdrop to Captain Marvel

400.q: Who is Adam Warlock?
400.a: A perfect artificial being created by the Sovereign — has a complex comics history with Thanos

400.q: What happened to Steve Rogers at the end of Endgame?
400.a: He went back in time to be with Peggy Carter, growing old and returning to give Sam the shield

400.q: What is the Civil War in Marvel comics?
400.a: Iron Man supports the Superhuman Registration Act; Cap opposes it — heroes fight each other

400.q: Who is the Beyonder in Marvel Comics?
400.a: An incredibly powerful cosmic entity from outside the Marvel universe — responsible for Secret Wars

400.q: What is the Living Tribunal?
400.a: A cosmic entity overseeing balance in the Marvel multiverse — above the Infinity Stones in power

400.q: What is the Savage Land?
400.a: A tropical environment hidden in Antarctica where dinosaurs and prehistoric creatures survived

What are all six Infinity Stones?
600.a: Space, Mind, Reality, Power, Time, and Soul

600.q: What is the Phoenix Force?
600.a: A cosmic entity bonding with a mutant host — most famously Jean Grey — granting near-unlimited power

600.q: Significance of Steve Rogers picking up Mjolnir in Endgame?
600.a: He was deemed worthy — Thor suspected this in Age of Ultron when Steve slightly budged it

600.q: What is the Eternals' true mission?
600.a: Created by Celestials to allow life to grow and energy to accumulate — for the Emergence: a new Celestial born inside Earth

600.q: Who is Galactus?
600.a: The Devourer of Worlds — a cosmic being who consumes planets for sustenance

600.q: What is the Negative Zone?
600.a: An anti-matter universe accessible through a portal — first explored by Mr. Fantastic

600.q: Who is the Molecule Man?
600.a: Owen Reece — can manipulate matter and energy at a molecular level — lynchpin of the entire multiverse in some storylines

600.q: Significance of the first Iron Man post-credits scene?
600.a: Nick Fury tells Tony he's not alone — the first hint of a shared Marvel Cinematic Universe

600.q: What is the Infinity War Decimation?
600.a: Thanos snapping with all six Infinity Stones, instantly wiping out 50% of all life in the universe

600.q: What is the concept of a Nexus Being?
600.a: A being who is a focal point of the universe's energy — Wanda Maximoff is the Nexus Being of Earth-616

600.q: What is the Multiverse in MCU terms and what opened it?
600.a: An infinite set of parallel universes — destabilised by the Infinity War snap and Strange's spell in No Way Home

600.q: What is Secret Invasion?
600.a: Skrulls have secretly replaced key figures in the Marvel universe — including heroes and politicians

600.q: What is the Rule of X in the Krakoa era?
600.a: The laws established for mutantkind on the sovereign nation Krakoa — from Jonathan Hickman's X-Men run

600.q: Who is Knull the God of Symbiotes?
600.a: The progenitor of all symbiotes — the darkness before creation itself — a Donny Cates villain

600.q: What is the Beyonder's Secret Wars?
600.a: He transports heroes and villains to Battleworld to fight — one of Marvel's most important crossover events

600.q: What is the significance of the Civil War II in Marvel comics?
600.a: The Inhuman Ulysses can predict the future — the debate over whether to act on future crimes splits the heroes again

600.q: Who is the One Above All?
600.a: The supreme being of the Marvel multiverse — the equivalent of God, overseeing all existence

600.q: What is the difference between Earth-616 and Earth-199999?
600.a: Earth-616 is the main Marvel Comics universe; Earth-199999 is the MCU

600.q: What is the significance of the snap reversal in Endgame?
600.a: Hulk used the gauntlet to reverse the snap — then Thanos's army arrived, leading to the final battle

600.q: What is the Celestials' role in Marvel cosmology?
600.a: Ancient cosmic beings who seeded life across the universe and return to judge whether planets should be destroyed
